description:
## What
Implement the three scheduled status management endpoints: GET /api/v1/scheduled_statuses (paginated list of pending scheduled posts for the authenticated persona); PUT /api/v1/scheduled_statuses/:id to reschedule (update scheduled_at, must still be >= now()+5min); DELETE /api/v1/scheduled_statuses/:id to cancel (delete row, must not leave orphaned delivery_queue rows).

## Why
Clients need to let users view, reschedule, and cancel queued posts. Without these endpoints the client UI for scheduled posts is read-only or broken.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social GET /api/v1/scheduled_statuses and GET /api/v1/scheduled_statuses/:id to capture exact JSON shape; note pagination header format (Link rel=next/prev); check what fields PUT /api/v1/scheduled_statuses/:id accepts
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add GET handler returning paginated ScheduledStatus list scoped to authenticated persona; add PUT handler validating new scheduled_at >= now()+5min, updating DB row; add DELETE handler removing post row and any delivery_queue rows with matching post_id atomically in a transaction
- Spawn a Test subagent to: test GET returns only own persona's scheduled posts (not another persona's); test PUT rejects past or near-future scheduled_at with 422; test DELETE removes post and asserts no delivery_queue rows remain; test 404 on unknown id

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v1/scheduled_statuses live response shape — note field names, pagination link header format
- Test approach: create scheduled post via POST in test setup, then exercise GET/PUT/DELETE; assert isolation between personas (persona A cannot see/modify persona B's scheduled posts); assert transaction atomicity of DELETE by checking delivery_queue table post-delete

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Scope all queries to authenticated persona's id — never expose other personas' scheduled posts
- DELETE must be atomic: delete post row and delivery_queue rows in one transaction so no orphan rows remain
- PUT must re-validate scheduled_at is still in the future by at least 5 minutes
- Return 404 (not 403) for ids that belong to a different persona (don't leak existence)
title: Implement GET/PUT/DELETE /api/v1/scheduled_statuses management endpoints
